text: Aurora Intel: Iran fired a cluster warhead ballistic missile at central Israel today, scattering bomblets over Petah Tikva and Bnei Brak — 6 wounded by shrapnel. Iran has now used cluster munitions on populated Israeli areas at least 5 confirmed times this conflict. Meanwhile Hezbollah killed a woman in her 30s at Mahanayim Junction with a rocket. Israel's total confirmed civilian deaths from this conflict now stand at 17.
verificationNote: Cluster warhead confirmed by JPost and Al Jazeera. Mahanayim death confirmed by MDA and Ynet.

text: CTP-ISW: Houthis struck Chinese-owned tanker MV Huang Pu with 6 anti-ship missiles; 1 direct hit, fire extinguished. Notably the vessel was carrying Russian crude oil. The Houthis remain operationally active despite not conducting the "major retaliation" campaign they promised when the Iran war began. Their targeting reflects opportunism, not a coherent campaign tied to Iran's operational calendar.
verificationNote: Houthi attack confirmed by FDD Long War Journal.

text: ISW Day 25 assessment: Trump credited Hegseth as the man who pushed hardest for the Iran strike, consistent with reporting that Hegseth pushed military options while Rubio handled diplomacy. Trump's approval rating fell to 36% — lowest of his second term. The 5-day ceasefire window closes March 28. Islamabad talks reportedly still being arranged despite Iran's public denials. Iran's new SNSC chief Zolghadr is a Basij hardliner, not a negotiator.
verificationNote: ISW assessment based on confirmed public reporting from multiple outlets.

text: Iran International: Despite all public denials, Araghchi privately signaled to Witkoff and Kushner that Mojtaba Khamenei has approved deal talks if conditions are met. But Iran's appointment of hardliner Zolghadr as SNSC chief signals the military faction is in control. Dual-track: negotiate privately, escalate publicly. Iran is trying to extract maximum concessions before publicly acknowledging any talks — a pattern consistent with 2015 JCPOA negotiations.
verificationNote: Iran International exclusive sourcing on Araghchi-Witkoff call. Zolghadr appointment confirmed by Bloomberg.

text: ELINT News: MAJOR — IDF strikes confirmed the IRGC's main central security headquarters in Tehran. This is separate from earlier strikes on Tehran IRGC regional HQ. Combined with the 82nd Airborne deployment and Iran's missile waves at Eilat and Jerusalem, Day 25 PM is the most kinetically active period of the afternoon watch so far.
verificationNote: IDF IRGC HQ strike confirmed by JNS and Alma Research Center.

text: OSINT Defender: Trump's "war has been won" claim is creating massive confusion. Iran's IRGC just called him a "deceitful president." Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf says the peace talk claims are "fakenews to manipulate oil markets." Yet Araghchi reportedly told Witkoff and Kushner he had Mojtaba Khamenei's blessing to close talks. Dual-track diplomacy: deny publicly, negotiate privately.
verificationNote: Araghchi-Witkoff back-channel reported by Iran International. Ghalibaf/IRGC public denials confirmed.

text: Combat Air Force Watch: The deployment of 82nd Airborne (2,000-3,000 paratroopers) to the Middle East alongside 2 MEUs (~5,000 Marines) gives CENTCOM an airborne assault capability that didn't previously exist in theater. This is not a defensive force. The 82nd Airborne is an offensive rapid-deployment division. Combined with the Hormuz reopening operation CENTCOM's Admiral Cooper is planning, ground options are being actively built.
verificationNote: Deployment facts confirmed. Tactical assessment is analyst commentary.

text: Babak Taghvaee: Bushehr strike confirmed by IAEA — a structure 350 metres from the reactor was destroyed. This is the second attack near Bushehr in one week. The IAEA reports no radiation release and no reactor damage. However, the escalatory signal is unmistakable: strikes are creeping closer to Iran's only nuclear power plant. Iran's Atomic Energy Organization has called for emergency IAEA Board action.
verificationNote: Bushehr proximity confirmed by IAEA and Haaretz Day 25.

text: Conflicts Watcher: Day 25 PM summary — Iran fired 13+ missile/drone barrages at Israel today including cluster warheads on central Israel. IDF destroyed IRGC central HQ and hit Bushehr proximity. US killed 15 PMF fighters in Iraq's deadliest US strike. 82nd Airborne deploying. Trump claims peace, Iran denies. Israel fears premature ceasefire. The conflict shows no signs of stopping despite diplomatic noise.
verificationNote: Summary sourced from confirmed reporting across TOI, JPost, AJ, CNN, NPR.

text: Alma Center daily report (March 24, 16:00): IDF struck central IRGC security HQ in Tehran embedded in civilian infrastructure. Overnight strikes hit missile sites in western/central Iran, energy facilities in Isfahan, Khorramshahr, Tabriz, Bandar Abbas, Sirjan. Confirmed elimination of ballistic missile expert Prof. Saeed Shamgadari, missile researcher Mohammad Navid Karishi, and Brig. Gen. Hamid Mirzadeh (senior missile array figure).
verificationNote: Alma Center daily report verified against IDF statements and JNS reporting.

text: Vantor satellite imagery confirms destruction of approximately a dozen IRIAF aircraft at Khatami Air Base northeast of Isfahan, including F-14 Tomcats. Hardened aircraft shelters penetrated consistent with GBU-28 or similar bunker-busting munitions. Iran's remaining operational fighter fleet estimated at fewer than 50 aircraft.
verificationNote: Satellite imagery analysis consistent with pre/post strike comparisons shared by multiple OSINT accounts.

text: CTP-ISW Day 25 evening update: Iranian missile attacks declined ~90% since strikes began. ~75% of ballistic missile launchers destroyed. Iran's daily launch rate dropped from 90/day to ~10/day. However Iran switching to cluster munitions which are penetrating Israeli air defenses more effectively — 12 injured in Bnei Brak cluster strike, 4 in Tel Aviv interception failure.
verificationNote: IDF launcher destruction stats confirmed by Time and JNS. Cluster munition analysis confirmed by Al Jazeera.

text: Unit 42 reports elevated Iranian cyber operations targeting Israel, US-aligned assets, and GCC states. Pro-Iran hacktivist groups and APT actors deploying Telegram-based C2 infrastructure to push malware. AWS Bahrain region disrupted again — second time since war began. Iran's cyber capabilities remain intact despite physical infrastructure losses.
verificationNote: AWS disruption confirmed by Al Jazeera. Unit 42 threat intel report published.

text: TankerTrackers: Only 6 AIS-visible vessels transited the Strait of Hormuz today vs 138/day pre-war. 7-day total just 16 crossings. CBS confirms at least 12 Iranian underwater mines (Maham 3 and Maham 7 limpet mines) in the strait. CENTCOM planning weeks-long mine-clearing operation. Iran's 'Ghost Fleet' of ~30 laden tankers still operating inside the Persian Gulf under IRGC protection.
verificationNote: Hormuz transit data confirmed by UANI shipping tracker. Mine reports confirmed by CBS News.

text: Intel Crab: Philippines becomes first country to declare national energy emergency over Iran war. Fuel prices up ~200%, only 45 days supply remaining. 4-day work week for civil servants. Kuwait airport hit by Iranian drones for the 4th time. German President Steinmeier calls war 'breach of international law' — trust in US 'lost.' Trump approval sinks to record-low 36%.
verificationNote: Philippines emergency confirmed by Rappler/Fortune. Kuwait strike confirmed by Gulf News. Steinmeier statement confirmed by France24. Trump approval confirmed by Reuters/Ipsos.

text: OSINTdefender: Israeli/US satellite imagery confirms Isfahan submarine R&D facility completely destroyed. IDF says this was Iran's only submarine development capability. Two cruise missile production sites in central Tehran also struck.

CTP-ISW: IRGC consolidating power with Zolqadr appointment as SNSC head. IDF strikes causing 'shock and confusion' within Iranian internal security apparatus. Latest assessment: IDF needs several more weeks to achieve war objectives.

TankerTrackers: Only 16 AIS-visible crossings in Hormuz in past 7 days vs ~966/week pre-war. Iran allowing selective passage for 'non-hostile' ships (China, India, Pakistan). Japan announces 30-day strategic oil reserve release — largest in history.

sentdefender: Rosatom chief declares Bushehr nuclear plant situation 'worst-case scenario.' Third evacuation phase begins — one group departed toward Armenian border. ~480 Russian personnel remain. IAEA urging 'maximum restraint.' Second strike on the facility in two weeks.

AuroraIntel: 15-point US ceasefire plan delivered to Iran via Pakistan's army chief. Iran military spokesman mocks: 'negotiating with yourselves.' Iran's counter-demands include US base closures and war reparations. Oil drops 6% on ceasefire hopes — Brent below $100 for first time since Day 22.

Intel Crab: Israel formally announces occupation of south Lebanon to Litani River — ~10% of country. Smotrich calls for annexation. 400,000 reservists authorized. 9 killed in Lebanon airstrikes today. Hezbollah anti-tank attack wounds IDF officer. Emergency extended to April 14.

Alma Center: Daily IDF update — submarine center destroyed, 2 cruise missile sites hit. Iran missile near Orot Rabin power plant missed. Saudi intercepts 32+ drones targeting Ras Tanura/Abqaiq. Southern Tehran residential strike kills 12. Senate war powers vote fails 47-53 for 3rd time.

Reuters: Hengaw 7th report documents 6,530 killed in Iran (640 civilians). Pentagon signs munitions surge deals with BAE/Lockheed/Honeywell. AP-NORC poll: 59% of Americans say military action excessive. $580M in pre-announcement oil bets under insider trading investigation.

OSINT: IRGC Wave 80 featured four successive missile salvos in 40 minutes — tracking shows Kheibar Shekan, Emad, and Sejjil ballistic missiles used. Wave also targeted US bases at Ali al-Salem/Arifjan (Kuwait), al-Azraq (Jordan), Sheikh Isa (Bahrain). IDF reporting multiple interceptions over Tel Aviv, Hadera, Bnei Brak; debris from one intercept caused vehicle fire in central Tel Aviv.
verificationNote: Confirmed by PressTV, Al Jazeera liveblog, Times of Israel liveblog.

text: IntelCrab: Iran has formally rejected the US 15-point peace plan and issued 5 counter-conditions via Araghchi. Key demand: international recognition of Iran's authority over Strait of Hormuz — this is a non-starter for US/Israel. Iran also demanding Vance replace Witkoff and Kushner in any talks. White House has rejected both demands. Gap between the two sides remains enormous.
verificationNote: PressTV, Anadolu Agency, NPR confirmed the 5-condition rejection. Daily Beast confirmed Vance demand.

text: Alma Center: IDF Day 26 summary — Hezbollah anti-tank commander Hassan Kamal Halawi killed in Qaaqaait al-Jisr drone strike; 5th and final Litani bridge destroyed (Katz confirms all 5 blown). US A-10s strike PMF in Anbar Province (7 killed, 10 wounded). Iraq NSC authorizes PMF self-defense response. Iran names Zolghadr as new SNSC secretary. Saudi Arabia/UAE reportedly moving toward war entry per WSJ.
verificationNote: Naharnet confirmed Halawi killing. Israel Hayom confirmed 5th bridge. Alma Research daily report March 25.

text: NPR: Philippines declares national State of Energy Emergency — first country globally to do so due to Iran war. 98% of oil from Gulf; fuel prices more than doubled. Gasoline reserves 53 days, diesel 46 days, LPG 23.5 days. 4-day work week for government ordered. Japan announces largest-ever strategic oil reserve release (30 days). Asia embracing energy austerity as Hormuz blockade enters Day 23.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Newsweek, UPI, and CNN all confirmed Philippines energy emergency.

text: Analysis: Iranian ballistic missile intercepted over Lebanese airspace for the first time — a significant tactical development. Iranian missiles are now routing through Lebanon's airspace to approach Israel from the north, potentially to avoid radar coverage gaps. IDF investigating two separate David's Sling failures (Dimona/Arad) with no connection found between incidents. Israel now has identified systematic gaps in northern approach corridors.
verificationNote: First Lebanese intercept confirmed by Times of Israel. David's Sling investigation confirmed by Washington Post.

text: UN SG Guterres: Declared the war 'out of control' — 'this has gone too far, the world is staring down the barrel of a wider war.' Appointed veteran French diplomat Jean Arnault as personal envoy for the conflict. Called on the US and Israel to end the war and on Iran to stop attacking neighbors. Called a direct US-Iran meeting 'the last opportunity to avoid a fully fledged escalation.'
verificationNote: UN News, Al Jazeera Day 26, and Pravda EN all confirmed Guterres statement and Arnault appointment.
